page.content: The table presents various parameters measured across three groups: DN (n = 100)mean ± SD, DM (n = 100)mean ± SD, and HC (n = 100)mean ± SD. In the DN group, the age is reported as 61.5 ± 8.5 years, with a duration of diabetes of 12.01 ± 7.6 years and a BMI of 29.09 ± 5.3 Kg/m2. The systolic blood pressure (SBP) is recorded at 125 ± 13.08 mmHg, and the diastolic blood pressure (DBP) at 77.2 ± 8.8 mmHg. The fasting blood sugar (FBS) level is 144.06 ± 54.3 mg/dl, while the HbA1C percentage is 7.2 ± 0.70%. Creatinine levels in this group are 1.2 ± 0.41 mg/dl, and urea levels are at 42.3 ± 17.3 mg/dl, with an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) of 65.9 ± 20.6 L/min/1.73 m2, and an albumin-to-creatinine ratio (ACR) of 83.3 ± 64.6 μg/mg. In the DM group, the age averages 57.36 ± 8.1 years, with a duration of diabetes of 10.20 ± 6.9 years and a BMI of 28.7 ± 4.5 Kg/m2. The SBP is 120.7 ± 20 mmHg, DBP is 76.5 ± 7.9 mmHg, FBS is 140.6 ± 45 mg/dl, HbA1C is 7.09 ± 0.73%, creatinine is 1.1 ± 0.27 mg/dl, urea is 36.7 ± 13.7 mg/dl, eGFR is 75.2 ± 20.7 L/min/1.73 m2, and ACR is 23.7 ± 5.5 μg/mg. In the HC group, age is 50.5 ± 10.7 years, BMI is 27.94 ± 4.9 Kg/m2, SBP is 115.2 ± 12.6 mmHg, DBP is 79.3 ± 6.2 mmHg, FBS is 92.4 ± 10.9 mg/dl, HbA1C is 5.5 ± 0.58%, creatinine is 1 ± 0.17 mg/dl, urea is 31.3 ± 10.1 mg/dl, eGFR is 78.8 ± 17.3 L/min/1.73 m2, and ACR is 21.3 ± 7.7 μg/mg. The p values for the parameters are denoted as *A for most comparisons and *B for eGFR and ACR.
